search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae pulmonary function spirometry guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk NG245 spirometry monitoring asthma recommendations
site:nice.org.uk NG115 COPD follow-up FEV1 FVC recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Device manuals do not support clinical interpretation or workflow content.
Medicine names, doses, and treatment algorithms were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: the exact sources are disease- and age-qualified, while the legacy workflow is a generic pulmonary-function review.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for chronic asthma and adult COPD spirometry assessment or monitoring, not every pulmonary-function indication.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local pulmonary-function laboratory, interpretation, and referral standards.
recency_verification: Current official NG245 and NG115 recommendations were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG245 and NG115 remain current official NICE guidance.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped spirometry, bronchodilator-response, respiratory-examination, and objective-test fields in suspected asthma.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped symptom correlation, inhaler or medicine use, smoking, oxygen or examination context, and monitoring fields in asthma.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult COPD FEV1, FVC, symptom, smoking, medicine-effect, oxygen, referral-need, and annual monitoring fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

unresolved_source_gaps: The workflow does not require asthma, COPD, adulthood, or a specific pulmonary-function indication.

Acute distress, interpretation of decline, obstruction or restriction, DLCO, respiratory rate, blood gas, chest radiography, generic clinician or medication plans, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some examination fields remain unsupported.
Test validity, interpretation, diagnosis, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
